system_prompt: You are an analyst. Read the brief carefully and provide your best-reasoned recommendation.
user_prompt:
You are presented with 5 anonymous responses (E1-E5) and 6 bipolar constructs. Rate each response on each construct on a 1-7 scale: 1 = strongly the LEFT pole, 7 = strongly the RIGHT pole, 4 = neutral or mixed.

Responses:
**E1:** # Recommendation: Option A — Deep AI Integration into the Flagship Product

With $8M and a 12-month window, the company's defensible asset is its existing customer base, workflows, and data. That's where capital compounds fastest and where AI disruption hits hardest. I'd put the bulk of the $8M into rebuilding core workflows with embedded LLM features.

**E2:** # Recommendation: Option A — Deep AI Integration into the Flagship Product

With $8M and a 12-month window, the company's overriding constraint is not capability, runway, or optionality—it is **relevance of the existing $50M ARR book to customers who are actively re-evaluating their software stack through an AI lens**. Every option except A defers that question. A confronts it directly.

**E3:** # Recommendation: Option A — Deep AI Integration into the Flagship Product

With $50M ARR and accelerating disruption, the company's most valuable and most vulnerable asset is its existing customer base. Those customers are already being pitched daily by AI-native startups promising to replace exactly the workflows your flagship owns. Every quarter without embedded AI in the core product is a quarter where renewal conversations get harder and competitive displacement risk compounds.

**E4:** # Recommendation: Option A — Deep AI Integration into the Flagship Product

With $50M ARR and accelerating disruption from both startups and Big Tech, the existential question isn't "can we build something new?" — it's "will our existing customers still need us in 18 months?" Every option except A leaves the flagship — the thing actually generating revenue and customer relationships — exposed to being hollowed out by competitors who embed AI natively into the workflows your users already perform elsewhere. The strategic logic is straightforward. You have three defensible assets: an installed c...

**E5:** # Recommendation: Option A — Deep AI Integration into the Flagship Product

With $50M ARR and accelerating disruption from both startups and Big Tech, the company's most valuable and most vulnerable asset is its existing customer base. Those customers are right now evaluating whether their incumbent productivity tool will remain competitive against AI-native challengers, or whether to switch. Every quarter without visibly embedded AI in the flagship is a quarter where renewal risk compounds and competitors anchor the "AI productivity" category in buyers' minds.

Constructs (id : left pole ↔ right pole):
  C_M1_1: 'Frames threat via customer/market-facing competitive dynamics (E2, E3)'  (1) ↔ (7)  'Frames threat via internal asset and capital logic (E1)'
  C_M1_2: 'Concise thesis stated and left to stand (E2, E3)'  (1) ↔ (7)  'Extended enumeration of defensible assets and logic (E4)'
  C_M1_3: 'Emphasizes renewal risk and category perception in buyers' minds (E3, E5)'  (1) ↔ (7)  'Emphasizes workflow hollowing-out by AI-native competitors (E4)'
  C_M5_1: 'Frames threat as customer renewal/competitive displacement risk'  (1) ↔ (7)  'Frames threat as customers needing flagship at all'
  C_M5_2: 'Emphasizes customer-facing competitive/disruption pressure'  (1) ↔ (7)  'Emphasizes internal asset compounding and capital logic'
  C_M5_3: 'Brief, assertion-driven rationale'  (1) ↔ (7)  'Extended, structured strategic argument'

OUTPUT FORMAT (strict). Write exactly 30 lines, no commentary, no markdown fences, no headers. One line per (construct, element) pair, in this exact format:
<construct_id>,<element>,<rating>

Example (showing the format only - your numbers will differ):
C_M1_1,E1,1
C_M1_1,E2,2
C_M1_1,E3,3
C_M1_2,E1,4
...

Constraints:
- rating is an integer from 1 to 7
- iterate all 6 constructs in the order listed, and within each construct all 5 elements in order (E1-E5)
- produce 30 lines total
- output ONLY the lines, nothing before or after
